page: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel x86_64 cores. It features hyperthreading, a 2.0 GHz clock speed, 16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core, and a bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD. The CPU includes a 16 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks fall into average and weak categories respectively. Large block uncached read and write speeds are limited to 35.47 GB/sec and 17.49 GB/sec. Cryptographic and physics calculations also show weaker performance profiles, while Redis SET operations maintain a stable average of 6.0 million operations per second.

The shared CPU allocation presents a resource-sharing tradeoff, making it less suitable for sustained heavy compute workloads. However, the inclusion of a fast 384 GB NVMe SSD and a balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio offers solid resource density for general-purpose workloads. This server is best suited for development environments, lightweight web serving, Redis caching, and basic database operations where peak multi-core performance is not continuously required.
description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an Intel x86_64 architecture with 2 physical cores and hyperthreading. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. The shared CPU allocation introduces resource-sharing tradeoffs, but the balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage provide good resource density. This server is well-suited for general-purpose applications, development environments, Redis caching, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 384 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-4c-16gb features 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 384 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A shared-core server balancing strong single-core performance with fast local NVMe storage for general-purpose and development workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared vCPUs on Intel x86_64 architecture with hyperthreading
16.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 384 GB NVMe SSD
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average Redis performance at 6.0 million operations per second
Suitable for general-purpose workloads, caching, and development environments
